description_long: This is a vintage portable radio receiver with a rectangular body, predominantly red with black and metallic accents. The front panel features a large speaker grille made of perforated metal, two prominent circular knobs for tuning and volume control, and a horizontal frequency display with green and yellow markings. The top of the radio has a sturdy black handle for carrying, and the sides show additional control dials and a textured finish. The back panel is mostly black, with technical information and a battery compartment cover visible. The overall design is utilitarian and robust, typical of mid-20th-century portable radios, and it would be at home in urban environments such as garages, workshops, or street markets.
description: A vintage red and black portable radio receiver with a metal speaker grille, tuning knobs, and a frequency display, featuring a sturdy carrying handle.
description_view_0: Front view showing the speaker grille, two control knobs, frequency display, and brand markings.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the red panel, a large tuning knob, and a smaller control dial.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the black back panel with technical information and a battery compartment cover.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a plain red panel and the edge of the handle visible.
